spec_type_rationale: pre-seeded from research handoff at research/2026-06-30-requestbar-visually-drifts-from/handoff.json; presentational-fidelity pass on the existing RequestBar organism + shared Dropdown panel, consistent with prior fidelity spec 010

findings.content: Only Shell writes documentElement data-mstyle; method per-verb colour MUST stay on the .method/.{METHOD} class path — RequestBar never writes data-mstyle.
Never use inline styles — class-based styling composed with cx(); all geometry/treatment edits land in RequestBar.css + Dropdown.css + className/markup, no inline style={{}}.

Prefer design tokens (CSS custom properties in tokens.css) over literal style values; bind shadow/radius/colour/padding to existing tokens (--shadow-lg/--radius-md/--radius/--bg-elev/--bg-hover/--border/--border-strong/--font-mono/--text-muted).
Reuse the existing link Icon atom and the canonical .method/{METHOD} verb-colour class path; introduce no new SVG or colour mechanism.

RequestBar .method{background:var(--bg-elev)} override breaks data-mstyle='chip' (white-on-white); CT pinned only 'soft' so a chip regression shipped — fidelity CT must cover all mstyle variants, not just soft.
Playwright CT fidelity tests must reproduce the full styling context (tokens.css import + data-mstyle + production className scope like .request-bar) or computed styles diverge from production.

Radix Dropdown (method menu) click-outside CT must wait for overlay readiness + yield one setTimeout(0) macrotask before page.mouse.click, else the dismiss listener races the arm (intermittent false pass).
RequestBar no-reflow CT must baseline with a non-empty URL; empty→filled conflates canSend-gated keycap mount with URL-length reflow → false regression.
Type-check = npm run typecheck:node && npm run typecheck:web; lint = eslint --cache .; build = npm run typecheck && electron-vite build — the hygiene verification commands for touched renderer files.

When a feature has design/reference.html, /breakdown produces a design-manifest.json + a static design-token provenance check backs the Design Fidelity principle; this feature has design/reference.html so the manifest gate applies.
RequestBar is a renderer-process React organism; renderer imports no Node/Electron and styles via tokens.css custom properties — change stays renderer-only.

HAZARD: .request-bar .request-bar__method.method (0,3,0) deliberately omits color so per-method text colour falls through the [data-mstyle] cascade; adding any color there kills all 7 per-method colours with no compile error — method-trigger tidy must not add color.

Seven (0,5,0) [data-mstyle='chip'] per-method counter-rules in RequestBar.css must stay in lockstep with METHODS in httpMethods.ts; touching method-trigger CSS risks the white-on-white chip regression if counter-rules drift.
Dropdown is a Radix-wrapped molecule; every CT that dismisses it needs the two-step readiness gate (await animations + setTimeout(0)).
Styling is semantic class names bound to tokens.css with per-component sibling CSS, no inline styles; the Dropdown panel treatment lives in molecules/Dropdown.css (shared).
RequestBar.tsx = method Dropdown + URL input + Send/Save/Share row wired to tabsStore.updateActiveSpec/markClean; RequestBar.css carries method-chip color classes + action-button states.
Dropdown.tsx is a controlled menu over Radix DropdownMenu; Dropdown.css holds its panel styles (reduced-motion-gated animation) — the OPEN PANEL surface to align.
The project icon set (icons.ts) already contains a 'link' icon — reuse via <Icon name=link> for the URL leading icon, no new SVG.

design/styles.css is the source of truth for RESOLVED reference values; copy the VALUES never the code; var(--token) ⇒ assert resolves-to-token; feed numbers into AC computed-style targets + CT getComputedStyle assertions.

Every required target token already exists: --shadow-lg, --radius-md (9px), --radius (7px), --bg-elev (#fff), --bg-hover, --border, --border-strong, --font-mono, --text-muted, --text — no token additions needed.
--text-muted/--text-faint were darkened 2026-06-30 for WCAG AA; the .btn-ghost rest colour binds --text-muted so Save/Share rest text inherits the new value automatically.

Reference .method-select: display flex, gap 6px, padding 7px 10px 7px 12px, border 1px var(--border), background var(--bg-elev), border-radius var(--radius), font-family var(--font-mono), font-weight 700, font-size 11.5px, letter-spacing 0.04em, min-width 88px; :hover border-color var(--border-strong); .method child font-size 11.5px flex:1. No justify-content.

Reference .url-bar: flex container (flex:1, align-items center), border 1px var(--border), background var(--bg-elev), border-radius var(--radius), height 32px, padding 0 12px, gap 6px, font-family var(--font-mono); :focus-within accent border + box-shadow 0 0 0 3px accent14%; input font-size 12.5px.

Reference .btn-ghost: color var(--text-muted), border-color var(--border), background var(--bg-elev); :hover color var(--text) + border-color var(--border-strong). Both Save and Share map to .btn-ghost; Save hover target = text→--text + border→--border-strong.

Reference dropdown panel: background var(--bg-elev), border 1px var(--border), border-radius var(--radius-md), box-shadow var(--shadow-lg), padding 4px, gap 1px. .dd-item: padding 6px 8px, gap 8px, border-radius var(--radius-sm), min-height 28px; :hover background var(--bg-hover).

Five un-enumerated drifts (method-trigger font/colour/treatment, method dropdown OPEN PANEL, URL link-icon+font+placeholder, Save hover, Share icon-only) are token-APPLICATION + presentational markup local to RequestBar.css/.tsx + Dropdown.css — not a token gap or logic defect; addresses hypotheses A+C, NOT B (no tokens.css change).

RequestBar.tsx:285 is a bare <input class=request-bar__url> with no .url-bar container/leading icon; restructure into a .url-bar flex wrapper with a leading <Icon name=link> + the input (gap 6px); icon exists at icons.ts:72.
RequestBar.tsx:290 placeholder is 'Enter URL'; target literal is EXACTLY 'Enter URL or paste cURL command…' (text only, NO cURL parsing behaviour). One-string edit.

RequestBar.tsx:339-341 renders <Icon name=share> + the text 'Share'; reference is icon-only. Fix = drop the text node only; .request-bar__share CSS already matches .btn-ghost; keep disabled (no behaviour assertion).

findings.source_section: Codebase Findings — method trigger + Save hover (hypothesis C)
findings.content: Method trigger (RequestBar.css:73) largely matches reference but has an extra justify-content:center (line 87) not in the reference — remove it; Save hover (RequestBar.css:296) appears already reference-correct. Both need a runtime computed-style confirm (residual drift = context-only, not a static value gap).

Dropdown.css:45 box-shadow is var(--shadow-md) → reference var(--shadow-lg); .dropdown-item padding .375rem .625rem (6px 10px) → reference 6px 8px; reference panel adds gap:1px (absent). Minor shadow/pad/gap rebind on the SHARED Dropdown molecule.

Dropdown is a SHARED molecule (prod consumers RequestBar + PrimitivesDemo); the panel rebind ripples to PrimitivesDemo + visual snapshots but is bounded and explicitly in the ticket scope — not an unbounded design-system gap.

010 enumerated bar geometry, URL radius+focus ring, method-select treatment, Send weight/shadow/keycap, and gave BOTH Save AND Share VISIBLE text labels (AC-11). 012 REVERSES Share to icon-only — a direct change to 010 AC-11; Save keeps its label, Share drops it.

010 deferred restoring the un-gated tokens.css .method colour defaults (hypothesis B) to a separate follow-up; 012 also does NOT do it — the in-app pill is coloured via Shell default data-mstyle='soft'.

010 verified fidelity via computed-style EXACT-equality Playwright CT + thresholded screenshot diff (explicit anti-aliasing pixel-threshold); 012 reuses the same tier and the same tokens.css + data-mstyle=soft fixture scope + Radix two-step dismiss gate.

009 behaviour to preserve unchanged: onSend intent, ⌘↵/⌘S shortcuts (preventDefault), canSend empty-after-trim guard, dirty/markClean, updateActiveSpec write path, per-tab selector isolation, Share disabled/no-op stub in final slot.

Method selector is the controlled 001 Dropdown (trigger styled .method/.{METHOD}, 7 methods from httpMethods.ts); URL is a single-line plain <input> bound to RequestSpec.url with no {{variable}}-highlight editor (epic D out of scope).

URL placeholder/font drift sits on the existing plain input; the .url-bar restructure must keep the input bound to RequestSpec.url and the canSend trim guard reading the same value — no behavioural change to the input binding.

decision_points.description: Fix the method dropdown OPEN PANEL by editing the SHARED Dropdown molecule (ripples to dev-only PrimitivesDemo + visual-snapshot baselines) now, or defer the panel to a separate design-system pass
decision_points.user_answer: include-dropdown-panel-now: align the shared Dropdown molecule panel (--shadow-lg, item padding 6px 8px, panel gap 1px) in this feature; bounded ripple to dev-only PrimitivesDemo + visual-snapshot baselines is accepted, matching the ticket scope.

decision_points.description: 010 gave BOTH Save and Share visible text labels; 012 makes Share icon-only — keep Save's visible label (only Share goes icon-only) or make both actions icon-only
decision_points.user_answer: save-keeps-label-share-icononly: only Share drops its text label (icon-only, stays disabled); Save remains a labelled .btn-ghost button and receives only its hover-state fix.

decision_points.description: no relevant decision point for data_flow_state
no relevant decision point for edge_cases
no relevant decision point for ui_ux_details
no relevant decision point for breaking_changes
no relevant decision point for tooling_configuration

mandatory_reads.n_a_reason: Presentational fidelity pass — no entity/type changes; RequestSpec.method stays HttpMethod, no new types introduced.
Desktop renderer UI change — no REST/GraphQL operations; zero network/IPC surface touched.

overview: Close the remaining visual-fidelity gap between the RequestBar organism (features 009/010, both Complete) and design/reference.html on five presentational props the 010 fidelity pass did not enumerate: the method-trigger font/colour/treatment, the method dropdown's OPEN PANEL styling, the URL input (a missing leading link icon, the wrong font, and a non-matching placeholder string), the Save button's hover state, and the Share button (rendered with a text label instead of icon-only). Each listed element is rebound to design/reference.html's resolved values (from design/styles.css + design-fidelity-contract.md) using existing tokens.css custom-properties and the project's own semantic class names — no new tokens, no design-export cruft, and ZERO behaviour change. The fix is localized to RequestBar.css/.tsx plus a bounded alignment of the shared Dropdown molecule's open panel.
current_state: RequestBar (src/renderer/src/components/organisms/RequestBar.tsx + sibling RequestBar.css) renders the [method ▾][URL][Send/Save/Share] bar wired to tabsStore (009/010, both Complete). Five presentational props still drift from design/reference.html. (1) The URL field is a bare input (src/renderer/src/components/organisms/RequestBar.tsx:285-292) with no leading icon and placeholder "Enter URL" (line 290) — the reference .url-bar (design/styles.css:772-795) is a flex container [leading icon + input, gap 6px] whose placeholder is "Enter URL or paste cURL command…". (2) Share renders an Icon plus a visible "Share" text node (src/renderer/src/components/organisms/RequestBar.tsx:339-342) — the reference shows it icon-only. (3) The method trigger uses cx('request-bar__method','method',method) (src/renderer/src/components/organisms/RequestBar.tsx:269); per docs/architecture.md its .request-bar .request-bar__method.method rule (specificity 0,3,0) carries an extra justify-content:center not present in reference .method-select (design/styles.css:749-770; mono / weight 700 / 11.5px / letter-spacing 0.04em / min-width 88px / padding 7px 10px 7px 12px / border --border / bg --bg-elev / --radius), and it deliberately omits `color` so the per-method colour falls through the [data-mstyle] cascade. (4) Save is a labelled .btn-ghost button whose hover treatment targets reference .btn-ghost:hover (design/styles.css:825-828: color --text, border-color --border-strong). (5) The shared Dropdown molecule's open panel — .dropdown-content (src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Dropdown.css:37-51) uses box-shadow var(--shadow-md) and .dropdown-item padding 0.375rem 0.625rem (6px 10px, Dropdown.css:85) with no inter-item gap — vs reference .dropdown (design/styles.css:1536-1566): box-shadow var(--shadow-lg), .dd-item padding 6px 8px, panel gap 1px (border-radius --radius-md, bg --bg-elev, hover bg --bg-hover already match). The "link" Icon already exists in atoms/icons.ts (icons.ts:72). Every required target value already exists as a token (--shadow-lg / --radius-md / --radius / --bg-elev / --bg-hover / --border / --border-strong / --font-mono / --text-muted) per design-fidelity-contract.md — no token additions. The Dropdown is a shared molecule (production consumers: RequestBar + dev-only PrimitivesDemo). Per the docs/architecture.md hazard, .request-bar__method.method must keep omitting `color`, and the seven (0,5,0) [data-mstyle='chip'] per-method counter-rules in RequestBar.css must stay in lockstep with METHODS in httpMethods.ts. The 010 fidelity CT (src/renderer/src/components/organisms/__tests__/RequestBar.ct.tsx) uses computed-style EXACT-equality assertions + a thresholded screenshot diff over a tokens.css + data-mstyle='soft' fixture with the Radix two-step dismiss gate; Shell is the sole writer of data-mstyle.
desired_behavior: RequestBar and the shared Dropdown open panel shall match design/reference.html on the five enumerated props, verified by computed-style EXACT-equality in Playwright component tests plus a thresholded screenshot diff (per the 005/010 fidelity approach), with ZERO behaviour change. (1) URL input: restructure into a .url-bar flex container holding a leading aria-hidden link Icon plus the existing input, binding the reference treatment (border 1px --border, background --bg-elev, border-radius --radius, height 32px, padding 0 12px, gap 6px, font-family --font-mono; :focus-within accent border + a 0 0 0 3px accent-14% ring) while keeping the input mono at 12.5px; the input stays bound to RequestSpec.url via updateActiveSpec and the canSend trim guard reads the same value. (2) URL placeholder: exactly "Enter URL or paste cURL command…" — literal text only, with NO cURL parsing or any other behaviour. (3) Share: drop the visible "Share" text node so it renders icon-only; because the visible text previously supplied the accessible name, restore an accessible name (aria-label "Share") so the icon-only button stays named, and keep it the 009 disabled/no-op stub in its final slot. (4) Save: keep its visible "Save" label and bind the reference .btn-ghost rest + hover treatment (rest: color --text-muted, border-color --border, background --bg-elev; hover: color --text, border-color --border-strong). (5) Method trigger: present the reference .method-select treatment (font-family --font-mono, weight 700, font-size 11.5px, letter-spacing 0.04em, padding 7px 10px 7px 12px, min-width 88px, border 1px --border, background --bg-elev, border-radius --radius) and REMOVE the extra justify-content:center; the rule continues to omit `color` so the per-method colour falls through the .method/.{METHOD} cascade — RequestBar never writes data-mstyle, and the seven chip counter-rules stay in lockstep with METHODS. (6) Method dropdown open panel: rebind the shared .dropdown-content to box-shadow var(--shadow-lg), set the inter-item gap to 1px, and set .dropdown-item padding to 6px 8px — keeping border-radius --radius-md, background --bg-elev, and the existing data-highlighted hover background --bg-hover. The shared-panel rebind ripples to dev-only PrimitivesDemo + the visual-snapshot baselines (accepted in scope). All fidelity is rebuilt from semantic classes bound to tokens.css (values from design/styles.css), NEVER by importing design-export cruft (data-om-*, __OmT, inline styles, tweaks-panel). All 009/010 behaviour is untouched (RequestSpec read/write, onSend, ⌘Enter/⌘S, canSend trim guard, dirty/markClean, per-tab isolation, Shell as sole writer of data-mstyle, the Send ⌘↵ keycap, and Save's labelled state); existing unit + CT suites stay green with new computed-style fidelity CT added; typecheck + lint + build pass.

affected_areas.impact: Modify — presentational only: wrap the URL input in a .url-bar flex container with a leading aria-hidden <Icon name=link>; set placeholder to the exact reference string; drop the Share visible text node (icon-only) and restore aria-label='Share'; keep the Save label. No logic change.

Modify — bind reference values via existing tokens: .url-bar container treatment + focus ring; method-select treatment with the extra justify-content:center removed and color still unset; .btn-ghost Save rest+hover. All rules scoped under .request-bar; the seven chip counter-rules stay in lockstep with METHODS.

Modify — rebind the open panel to reference values: .dropdown-content box-shadow var(--shadow-lg) + 1px inter-item gap; .dropdown-item padding 6px 8px. Bounded ripple to dev-only PrimitivesDemo + visual-snapshot baselines.

Create/extend — add Playwright CT computed-style EXACT-equality assertions on the five enumerated props + a thresholded screenshot diff; reuse the tokens.css + data-mstyle=soft fixture scope and the Radix two-step dismiss gate; rebaseline the Dropdown panel visual snapshot; existing behaviour/unit suites stay green.

acceptance_criteria.statement: The RequestBar and Dropdown source shall contain no design-export cruft markers such as data-om attributes, __OmT wrappers, or a tweaks-panel.
The renderer shall provide a link icon in the project icon set for reuse as the URL leading icon.

WHEN the user operates any RequestBar control or keyboard shortcut, the RequestBar shall preserve the feature-009/010 behaviour unchanged including the updateActiveSpec write path, the onSend intent, the Cmd-Enter and Cmd-S shortcuts, markClean on Save, and the canSend empty-after-trim guard.
WHILE the active tab changes, the RequestBar shall render the newly active tab method and url without leaking the previous tab values.
The RequestBar shall keep Shell as the sole writer of the document data-mstyle attribute and shall never write it.
WHILE Share is rendered, the RequestBar shall keep it a disabled no-op stub in its final slot.
The RequestBar URL input placeholder shall be exactly "Enter URL or paste cURL command…" with no cURL parsing or any other behaviour.
WHILE Share is rendered, the RequestBar shall present it icon-only with no visible text node and shall carry an accessible name of Share via aria-label.

WHEN Save is hovered, the RequestBar shall present the reference btn-ghost hover treatment of color var(--text) and border-color var(--border-strong), keeping its visible Save text label and the rest treatment of color var(--text-muted), border-color var(--border), background var(--bg-elev).

WHILE the method dropdown panel is open, the renderer shall present the reference panel treatment of box-shadow var(--shadow-lg), a 1px inter-item gap, and dropdown-item padding of 6px 8px, keeping border-radius var(--radius-md), background var(--bg-elev), and the existing highlighted hover background var(--bg-hover).

WHEN the fidelity suite runs, the enumerated computed-style properties for the url-bar, method-select, Save hover, icon-only Share, and dropdown panel shall be asserted by exact equality in a real browser via Playwright component tests backed by a thresholded screenshot diff within an explicit anti-aliasing pixel-threshold.

WHILE the URL field is rendered, the RequestBar shall present it as a url-bar flex container holding a leading aria-hidden link icon and the input bound to the active request url, with the reference border, elevated background, var(--radius) corner, fixed height, horizontal padding, inter-element gap, and mono font from design/styles css, plus an accent border and an accent ring on focus-within.

WHILE the method trigger is rendered, the RequestBar shall present the reference method-select treatment of mono font at bold weight with the reference font-size, letter-spacing, padding, and min-width, var(--border) border, var(--bg-elev) background, and var(--radius) corner, with the extra justify-content center removed and color left unset so the per-method colour falls through the method class cascade.

The touched RequestBar and Dropdown source shall document the url-bar restructure, the icon-only Share with restored aria-label, the method-trigger no-color and no-justify rule, and the shared Dropdown panel rebind in comments.
The renderer source shall pass strict type-checking.
The codebase shall pass ESLint without new errors.
The touched RequestBar source shall contain no inline style attributes outside comments.
The RequestBar and Dropdown unit and component test suites shall pass.

ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI pipeline changes; tests run via existing npm scripts (vitest/playwright), not a new pipeline stage.
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No new git hooks or commit gates; the project existing forge gates apply unchanged.

out_of_scope.content: No tokens.css/tokens.json change — every reference value already exists as a token; restoring the un-gated tokens.css .method colour defaults (research hypothesis B, deferred by 010) is NOT in this feature.

No behaviour change whatsoever — HTTP execution, the response view, RequestSpec read/write semantics, onSend, the Cmd-Enter/Cmd-S shortcuts, dirty/markClean, the canSend guard, and per-tab isolation are all excluded; this is presentational/CSS only.
No cURL behaviour — the URL placeholder mentions cURL as literal text only; no paste-to-import, parsing, or any other cURL handling is implemented.
The Send split-dropdown reference section is DEVIATE/OUT — not implemented in this feature.

No chrome outside the RequestBar and the method Dropdown open panel — Params/Auth/Headers/Body/Tests/Code sub-tabs, {{variable}} highlighting, the environment selector, the command palette, and the app header are untouched; no dark-theme-specific geometry; no repo-wide reformatting.

constraints.content: Only Shell writes documentElement data-mstyle (§4 Never / 009 AC-6)
Never use inline styles — class-based styling via cx() (§4 Never)
Prefer design tokens over literal values (§4 Prefer)
Only Shell writes documentElement data-mstyle; the method per-verb colour stays on the .method/.{METHOD} class path and the RequestBar never writes data-mstyle.
Never use inline styles — class-based styling composed with cx(); all geometry and treatment edits land in RequestBar.css, Dropdown.css, and className/markup.

Prefer design tokens over literal values; bind shadow/radius/colour/padding to existing tokens (--shadow-lg, --radius-md, --radius, --bg-elev, --bg-hover, --border, --border-strong, --font-mono, --text-muted); raw px only where no token exists (item padding, panel gap).

Style exclusively via tokens-bound semantic class names; copy the reference VALUES from design/styles.css, never the export code; var(--token) asserts resolves-to-token; never reproduce design-export cruft (data-om-*, __OmT, inline styles, tweaks-panel).

Scope all RequestBar fidelity CSS under the .request-bar selector; the shared Dropdown panel rebind is the only intentionally cross-component change; keep the seven (0,5,0) [data-mstyle=chip] per-method counter-rules in lockstep with METHODS in httpMethods.ts.

Verify fidelity via computed-style EXACT equality in Playwright CT plus a thresholded screenshot diff; CT fixtures must import tokens.css, set data-mstyle on the host, apply the Radix two-step dismiss gate, baseline the no-reflow check with a non-empty URL, and cover mstyle variants beyond soft.

The change is renderer-only (no Node/Electron) and presentational; reuse the existing link Icon atom and the canonical .method/{METHOD} path; introduce no new tokens, no new colour mechanism, and no behaviour change (addresses research hypotheses A and C, not B).
This feature has design/reference.html, so /breakdown emits a design-manifest plus a static token-provenance check; every token binding must trace to a design/styles.css resolved value.

All feature-009/010 behaviour must not regress — onSend, the Cmd-Enter/Cmd-S shortcuts, the canSend empty-after-trim guard, dirty/markClean, updateActiveSpec, per-tab render isolation, Shell as sole writer of data-mstyle, the Send keycap, and the labelled Save state.

open_questions.content: The exact screenshot-diff anti-aliasing pixel-threshold (Playwright toHaveScreenshot threshold + maxDiffPixelRatio) is deferred to /plan; the spec mandates an explicit threshold MUST be set (no eyeballing), mirroring the 010 Q-2 precedent.

Runtime-confirm via a design-auditor computed-style + screenshot diff that the method-trigger and Save-hover already meet the design/styles.css targets (010 may have fixed them) before locking those ACs to concrete values; residual drift, if any, is context-only, not a static value gap.

risks.risk: Behaviour paths untouched. Main risk is the shared Dropdown molecule ripple (PrimitivesDemo + visual snapshots) and CT fidelity needing the data-mstyle=soft context (005 fixture-scoping).
The shared Dropdown panel rebind (shadow/padding/gap) ripples to dev-only PrimitivesDemo and the visual-snapshot baselines, risking an unrelated dropdown regression or a snapshot failure.
risks.mitigation: Limit the edit to box-shadow + item padding + panel gap; rebaseline the Dropdown visual snapshot deliberately; the only other prod consumer (PrimitivesDemo) is dev-only and tree-shaken from production.

risks.risk: The method-trigger CSS edit could reintroduce the white-on-white chip regression if the seven chip counter-rules drift from METHODS, or kill all per-method colours if a color declaration is accidentally added.
risks.mitigation: Keep color unset on .request-bar__method.method; keep the seven (0,5,0) chip counter-rules in lockstep with METHODS; assert the per-method computed colour across mstyle variants (not just soft) in CT.

risks.risk: Method-trigger and Save-hover may already match the reference from 010; locking ACs to assumed values without a runtime check could enshrine a wrong geometry/treatment.
risks.mitigation: Confirm via a design-auditor runtime computed-style + screenshot diff vs design/reference.html before locking those ACs; residual drift, if any, is context-only, not a static value gap.

risks.risk: CT flakiness — the Radix dismiss arm-race, the keycap-mount no-reflow baseline confound, and jsdom inability to resolve computed/pseudo styles can produce intermittent false pass/fail.
risks.mitigation: Author fidelity asserts in real-browser Playwright CT; apply the two-step dismiss gate; baseline the no-reflow check with a non-empty URL; clear playwright/.cache + node_modules/.vite + dist when a build/test error names a clean file.

risks.risk: Dropping Share's visible text label without restoring an accessible name would break existing getByRole name queries for the Share button.
risks.mitigation: Add aria-label Share when going icon-only; run the existing RequestBar CT + unit accessible-name queries as a regression gate.

approval_summary:
I've created the specification at `specs/012-requestbar-element-fidelity/spec.md`. Key points:
- **What changes**: Close the remaining visual-fidelity gap between the RequestBar organism (features 009/010, both Complete) and design/reference.html on five presentational props the 010 fidelity pass did not enumerate: the method-trigger font/colour/trea...
- **Files affected**: 6 files across 4 areas
- **Acceptance criteria**: 19 testable criteria across 5 AC categories
- **Out of scope**: No tokens.css/tokens.json change — every reference value already exists as a tok; No behaviour change whatsoever — HTTP execution, the response view, RequestSpec ; No cURL behaviour — the URL placeholder mentions cURL as literal text only; no p; …

Please review and either approve or request changes. Once approved, run `/plan` to create the technical implementation plan.
plan_handoff_block:
## Manual next step — run /plan

A structured handoff (specs/012-requestbar-element-fidelity/handoff.json) is written for /plan. /plan auto-discovers it on its first run and reads the upstream plan-seeds — but you still launch /plan manually (there is no auto-dispatch from /specify). Restart Claude Code (exit and relaunch the CLI/app so the newly installed command is picked up), then run the command below in this repo. The spec path is explicit so /plan does not need most-recent-spec discovery:

~~~
/plan specs/012-requestbar-element-fidelity/spec.md
~~~

Minimum handoff data:
- Spec status: Draft
- Spec type: feature_addition
- AC count: 19 across 5 subsections (5.1: 2, 5.2: 4, 5.3: 7, 5.4: 0, 5.5: 0, 5.6: 1, 5.7: 5)
- Decision-point coverage: 2 answered, 0 default-applied, 0 deferred-OOS, 0 deferred-open-question
- Affected areas: 4 across src
- Out-of-scope items: 5
- Open questions: 2
- Constraints: 13
- Risks: 6
- Phase 1.5 finding coverage: 100% (all findings landed)

Reference: specs/012-requestbar-element-fidelity/spec.md

open_question_resolutions.resolution_text: Plan sets the concrete Playwright screenshot-diff threshold in the fidelity CT (toHaveScreenshot maxDiffPixelRatio + threshold), mirroring the 010 CT; the exact number is a Key Design Decision in plan.md.
